nameMeanings.name: May
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English male personal name May, a pet form of Matthew (see Mayhew ).

English, Dutch, and German: from a personal name or nickname taken from the month of May (Middle English, Old French mai, Middle High German meie, from Latin Maius (mensis), from Maia, a minor Roman goddess of fertility). This name was sometimes bestowed on someone born or baptized in the month of May; it was also used to refer to someone of a sunny disposition, or who had some anecdotal connection with the month of May, such as owing a feudal obligation then. In England, this name was possibly also a pet form of Mary or Margaret. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Denmark.
English: nickname from Middle English mey, may ‘kinsman’.

variants.name: Mae
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Akihiro, Ichiro, Kenichiro, Wakako, Yoshikazu.
Japanese (pronounced as two syllables): written 前 ‘front’ or ‘before’. It is not common in Japan. Some occurrences in America could be shortened versions of longer names beginning with this element.
Hawaiian: unexplained.

variants.name: Mayland
meanings: English (Yorkshire):

habitational name from any of the places so called in Essex and Durham. The Essex placename may derive from Old English mægthe ‘mayweed’ + land ‘land’ or from Old English ēg-land ‘island’ with initial m- carried over from a preceding thǣm (the dative case of the definite article). The Durham placename probably derives from the surname May + Middle English land ‘land’. There may have been some confusion with Malin .
variant of   Malin  .

variants.name: Maye
meanings: Irish: from Ó Miadhaigh ‘descendant of Miadhach’, a personal name meaning ‘honorable, proud’.
English: variant of   May  .

variants.name: Maike
meanings: German: from a pet form of   May  .
History: Carl Maike (1853–1937) emigrated from Grabrow (Grabroh), Germany, to Alma, KS, in 1881.

variants.name: Majewski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Casimir, Janusz, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Andrzej, Tadeusz, Jacek, Krzysztof, Mariusz, Stanislaw, Zofia, Bronislawa.
Polish and Jewish (from Poland): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Majewo.
Jewish (from Poland): surname adopted with reference to the month of May, Polish maj, assimilated to the Polish surname (see May ).

variants.name: Makin
meanings: English (northwestern): from the Middle English personal name Maykin, a diminutive of May , a pet form of Mayhew (see Matthew ). Compare Makinson .
English (northwestern): nickname from Middle English maide + diminutive suffix -kin ‘little maid’, perhaps used in a derogatory sense.
Irish: variant of   Mackin  .

variants.name: Maj
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Agnieszka, Jaroslaw, Wieslaw, Augustyn, Janusz, Jozef, Katarzyna, Krystyna, Krzysztof, Maciej, Piotr, Tadeusz.
Polish and Jewish (from Poland): surname adopted with reference to the month of May, Polish maj (see May 2). Compare May 9.
Jewish (from Poland): Polish-influenced variant of   May   8.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Mayes
meanings: English: variant of May 1, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Maio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Marco, Antonio, Carmela, Domenic, Rocco, Abilio, Attilio, Biagio, Carlo, Dante, Emilio, Francesco, Geremia, Gino, Rolando, Ruben.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from a personal name or nickname based on the word for the month of May, Latin Maius (see May 2).
Italian: possibly also from the personal name Maio of ancient Germanic origin (from magu- ‘boy’).

nameMeanings.name: Johannes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: "Pancraz"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Pancras .

en.lifeSummary: Johannes "Pancraz" May was born in 1400, in Sachsen, Sachsen bei Ansbach, Ansbach, Bavaria, Germany. He had at least 1 son. He died after 1435, in Devon, England, United Kingdom.
